title: Viseur AI Wins ITU AI for Good Innovation Factory Türkiye — Healthcare Workflow AI From Ankara to the World
detail: Ankara-based healthcare technology startup Viseur AI won the ITU AI for Good Innovation Factory Türkiye competition on April 21, 2026, winning the right to represent Turkey at the AI for Good Global Summit in Geneva in July 2026 and compete for the $20,000 grand prize. Viseur AI's platform integrates advanced machine learning and deep learning into clinical workflows — seamlessly connecting with hospital information systems (HIS), radiology information systems (RIS), laboratory systems (LIS), imaging archives (PACS), and pharmacy information systems (PIS) — enabling faster and more accurate diagnoses while reducing administrative burden on healthcare professionals. The no-code AI development ecosystem for medical imaging allows healthcare professionals to build and deploy diagnostic AI models without programming expertise, lowering the barrier for AI adoption in settings with limited technical staff. CEO Serkan Sökmen co-founded the company in 2024 after experiencing healthcare workflow inefficiencies firsthand — including excessive documentation workloads, delayed diagnoses due to radiologist shortages, and complex data integration challenges — in the Turkish health system. The platform complies with international data standards (DICOM, HL7) and has certification under ISO 27001 (information security) and ISO 13485 (medical device quality), making it suitable for regulated healthcare environments. The win is significant for Turkey's emerging AI health sector and for the ITU AI for Good Innovation Factory's mission to identify and scale AI ventures addressing global humanitarian and health challenges. Viseur AI gains access to UN-supported startup acceleration including mentorship, investment connections, and global media exposure through the program.
